mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security, scalability, and standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks. Use Kagenti when you need a robust solution for managing AI agents in production environments.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is essential for managing your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.
commands: gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step installs Kagenti using Helm, deploying it to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backs up the current state of your Kagenti deployment.
> Upgrades the Kagenti release to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kagenti deployment failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements: 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the application inside the pod fails to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and enhanced security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
